Know Your Stone

From earth to your kitchen.

Every material tells a different story. Find the one that fits your lifestyle, budget, and vision.

Granite
Natural Igneous Rock — Worldwide Origins
Basic → Mid $40 – $120 / sq ft

Pros

  • Extremely durable & heat resistant
  • Unique natural patterns
  • Scratch resistant surface
  • Increases home value
  • Wide variety of colors
  • Cost-effective entry point

Cons

  • Requires annual sealing
  • Porous — can stain if unsealed
  • Heavy — needs reinforced base
  • Inconsistent slab pattern
  • Limited uniformity
Best for Busy kitchens & high-traffic areas
Get a Quote
Quartzite
Natural Metamorphic Rock — Brazil, India
Premium → Exotic $70 – $200+ / sq ft

Pros

  • Harder than granite
  • Marble-like appearance
  • Heat & scratch resistant
  • UV color-stable outdoors
  • Prestigious natural look
  • Long-lasting investment

Cons

  • Higher cost than granite
  • Needs periodic sealing
  • Difficult to fabricate
  • Often confused with marble
  • Limited color palette
Best for Luxury kitchens & outdoor counters
Get a Quote
Marble
Natural Metamorphic Rock — Italy, Greece, Turkey
Premium → Exotic $60 – $250+ / sq ft

Pros

  • Timeless, elegant aesthetic
  • Cool surface — ideal for baking
  • Each slab is one-of-a-kind
  • Adds premium resale value
  • Unmatched classic beauty
  • Develops unique patina over time

Cons

  • Soft — etches from acids
  • High maintenance & sealing
  • Scratches more easily
  • Stains from wine, coffee, oil
  • Not ideal for heavy daily use
Best for Bathrooms, low-use kitchens & décor
Get a Quote
Engineered
Quartz
93% Natural Quartz + Resin Binders
Mid-Range $50 – $150 / sq ft

Pros

  • Non-porous — no sealing needed
  • Highly stain resistant
  • Consistent color & pattern
  • Low maintenance
  • Hygienic surface
  • Huge design variety

Cons

  • Not heat resistant — use trivets
  • Can fade in direct sunlight
  • Not a fully natural product
  • Seams may be visible
  • Lower resale prestige vs. stone
Best for Modern kitchens & family homes
Get a Quote

Signature Materials

Finished work. Real stone.

These are the materials we work with every day — sourced, cut, and installed by our team across West Texas.

Quartzite — Exotic Natural Stone

Exotic

Taj Mahal

Quartzite — Brazil

Warm cream and gold tones with soft veining. One of the most sought-after quartzites — elegant enough for luxury kitchens, durable enough to last generations. Ideal for full slabs and waterfall edges.

Exotic

Perla Venata

Quartzite — Brazil

Soft white background laced with delicate grey and pearl veining. A refined alternative to marble — with quartzite's superior hardness. Perfect for island tops and statement bathrooms.

Granite — Premium Natural Stone

Premium

Montecristo

Granite — Brazil

Striking wave-like layers of cream, beige, and silver-grey flow across the slab like a natural landscape. Montecristo's dramatic linear movement makes every installation a true statement piece — stunning on large islands and full kitchen suites.

Engineered Quartz — Design Versatility

Mid-Range

Calacatta Series

Engineered Quartz

Crisp white with bold grey veining engineered to mimic high-end marble — without the maintenance. Non-porous, consistent, and stunning. A family-friendly choice that never compromises on style.

Mid-Range

Urban Concrete

Engineered Quartz

Matte, industrial tone with subtle texture — popular in modern and minimalist kitchens. Available in charcoal, warm grey, and sand. Hides wear exceptionally well in high-traffic homes.

Outdoor Kitchens — Dolomite & Soapstone

Built for West Texas Outdoors

Heat. Sun. Wind.
No problem.

Dolomite and soapstone are two of the best natural stone choices for outdoor kitchens in hot, arid climates. Both handle direct sun, temperature swings, and outdoor grease without flinching — and both develop a beautiful natural character over time.

Outdoor Recommended

Dolomite

Natural Sedimentary Rock

Often white or light grey with soft, flowing veining. More heat-tolerant than marble with a similar refined look. Excellent for outdoor BBQ counters, bar tops, and covered patios. Requires sealing but holds up to outdoor conditions well.

Heat Tolerant UV Stable Sealing Required
Outdoor Recommended

Soapstone

Natural Metamorphic Rock — Brazil, USA

Dark charcoal grey stone with a silky, matte feel. Naturally non-porous — no sealing ever needed. Develops a rich patina with mineral oil treatments. Excellent heat resistance makes it ideal next to grills and outdoor fireboxes.

No Sealing Needed Extreme Heat Resistant Non-Porous
Why Border Stone

El Paso's stone specialists.

From the slab yard to your home, we handle every step with care and precision.

⬡
In-House Fabrication

We cut, edge, and finish every slab ourselves. No middlemen — faster turnaround and tighter quality control from start to finish.

◈
Two Yards, One Team

With locations in El Paso and Odessa, we serve the entire West Texas region — including Midland, Alpine, and Marfa — with the same craftsmanship and care at every stop.

◇
Free Estimates

No surprises. We visit your space, take precise measurements, and deliver a clear, itemized quote before a single cut is made.
